name: Quantitative Time-Resolved Mitotic Spindle Dynamics Profiling
description: You are a cell biologist studying mitotic spindle assembly dynamics from a 5D confocal time-lapse. Using the 'Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack)' sample in Fiji, perform a quantitative time-resolved analysis of per-cell spindle intensity. Split the hyperstack into individual channels, identifying which shows DNA/chromosomes and which shows the tubulin/spindle. Create max-intensity Z-projections for both channels preserving all time frames, saving as dna_max_projection.tif and tubulin_max_projection.tif. On the DNA projection at timepoint 1, segment individual nuclei using Gaussian Blur (sigma=2), Otsu thresholding, binary hole-filling, Watershed separation, and particle analysis (minimum size 100 sq pixels, exclude edge objects, add detected nuclei to the ROI Manager). Save the ROI set as nuclear_rois.zip. Using those nuclear ROIs, measure Mean, Area, and Integrated Density on the tubulin projection across all time slices via Multi Measure. Save as spindle_dynamics.csv. Create a temporal montage from the tubulin projection showing every 10th time frame (frames 1, 11, 21, 31, 41, 51) in a single row, saving as tubulin_montage.tif. All output files to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

metadata.image_source: Fiji built-in sample (File > Open Samples > Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack))
